x-sap-shortText: Enables you to create, schedule, monitor, and secure jobs using various authentication methods and scheduling options.

info.title: SAP Job Scheduling Service REST API
info.description:
REST APIs for the SAP Job Scheduling service that allows you to create, manage, and monitor jobs and job schedules.

The SAP Job Scheduling service provides options to:
- Create and manage jobs with HTTP endpoints or Cloud Foundry tasks
- Schedule jobs using cron expressions, one-time schedules, or recurring intervals
- Monitor job execution through detailed run logs
- Secure job actions with OAuth 2.0 authentication
- Configure alerts and monitoring integrations

## Authentication
The service supports multiple authentication mechanisms:
- **OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow** (service-to-service authentication for automated API access)
- **OAuth 2.0 JWT tokens** (direct token usage, recommended for standard plan)
- **Bearer tokens** (for Cloud Foundry environments)

### OAuth 2.0 Configuration
To use OAuth 2.0 flows in Swagger UI:
1. Click the "Authorize" button
2. Select the appropriate OAuth 2.0 flow
3. Enter your client ID and client secret
4. For Client Credentials flow, tokens are obtained automatically

**Note:** Replace `{landscape-region}` and `your-subdomain` in the OAuth URLs with your actual SAP BTP landscape domain and subdomain.

## Rate Limits
The API implements rate limiting with both absolute and client-specific limits:
- Requests may be throttled when approaching limits
- 429 (Too Many Requests) returned when client limits exceeded
- 503 (Service Unavailable) returned when absolute limits exceeded
- Retry-After header provided when requests are denied

## Time Zone
All timestamps are in UTC timezone. No other timezones are currently supported.

## Request Size Limits
POST request body size is limited to 100 KB.

servers.description: SAP Job Scheduling Service REST API Server

landscape-region.description: SAP BTP landscape region, you can check your credentials for Job Scheduler service instance for the correct region value.

post.description:
Creates a job by accepting one or more job schedules to be created.

**Note:** You cannot create CF tasks using the REST API because they require CF permissions. You can create them through the Dashboard.

The job creation request fails if a job with the same name already exists for one instance of the service - including multitenancy.

Always use HTTPS in the action endpoint URL to encrypt data during communication.

value.name: validateSalesOrder
value.description: cron job that validates sales order requests

Location.description: Relative resource URI for finding the job details

get.description:
Retrieves all jobs in a service instance with optional filtering and pagination.

Supports filtering using OData-style filter expressions with properties:
- name, subDomain, tenantId
- Boolean operators: and, or
- Function: contains
- Equality operator: eq

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of jobs to retrieve (default 10, max 200)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of jobs to skip (default 0, zero-based pagination)

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of specific job to retrieve

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of specific job to retrieve

parameters.name: jobType
parameters.description: Type of job to filter by

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID to filter jobs by

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
OData-style filter expression. Examples:
- `contains(name, 'my_job')`
- `name eq 'my_job'`
- `contains(name, 'test') and subDomain eq 'my_domain'`

get.description:
Retrieves the saved configuration settings of a specified job.
Optionally includes job schedules if displaySchedules parameter is true.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job to retrieve

parameters.name: displaySchedules
parameters.description: Whether to include schedule details in the response

put.description:
Updates an existing job's runtime information using job ID.
You can update one or more attributes of the job. If a parameter is not provided 
or is undefined, the existing value is retained.

**Note:** This endpoint does NOT support creating jobs. Use POST /jobs to create a new job,
or PUT /jobs/{name} to create/update by name.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job to update

value.message: Error while configuring job: Job Not Found
value.detailedError: Error while configuring job: Job Not Found

delete.description:
Deletes a job and all its runtime information such as schedules and logs.
Also terminates processing of all schedules (active and inactive) of the job.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job to delete

value.message: Error while removing job: Job Not Found
value.detailedError: Error while removing job: Job Not Found

put.description:
Configures a job with updated runtime information using job name.
If the job name already exists, the API replaces the job content with new content
retaining the same job ID and name.

If used to create a job, parameters must conform to the same constraints as the create job API.
The job name in the request URI must match the job name in the request body.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the job to update/create

value.name: newSuperCoolName
value.description: cron job that validates sales order requests

value.message: Error while configuring job: Extra scheduling parameters passed
value.detailedError: Error while configuring job: Extra scheduling parameters passed

post.description:
Creates a job schedule for a specified job.
The job configuration settings (Action URL, HTTP Method, and Job Activation Status) 
are valid for the new schedule.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job for which schedule is to be created

Location.description: Relative resource URI for finding the schedule details

get.description: Retrieves all schedules for a specified job with pagination support

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job whose schedules to retrieve

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of schedules to retrieve (default 10)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of schedules to skip (default 0, zero-based pagination)

delete.description:
Deletes all schedules of the specified job.
All related information such as schedule configurations and logs are deleted.
Processing of all schedules is immediately terminated.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of job whose schedules are to be deleted

post.summary: Activate or deactivate all job schedules
post.description: Activates or deactivates all existing schedules for a job

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job whose schedules are to be activated/deactivated

activationStatus.description:
Requested activation status:
- true: activate all schedules
- false: deactivate all schedules

get.description:
Retrieves the saved configuration settings of a specified job schedule.
Optionally includes schedule logs if displayLogs parameter is true.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job to which schedule belongs

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: ID of the schedule to retrieve

parameters.name: displayLogs
parameters.description: Whether to include logs in the response (max 200 logs)

put.description:
Configures/updates the runtime information of a job schedule for a specified job.
All job configuration settings remain valid for the modified schedule.

If a job schedule is in the execution queue, calling this API stops the execution process.
If the schedule was identified for execution, the updated schedule is executed immediately.

**Note:** The API does not support changing the original scheduling mode (e.g., from cron to repeatInterval).
However, changing the value for the existing scheduling parameter is supported.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job to which schedule belongs

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: ID of the schedule to update

delete.description:
Deletes the specified job schedule.
All related information such as job schedule configurations and logs are deleted.
The processing of the schedule is also immediately terminated.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: ID of the job schedule to delete

get.description:
Retrieves the run logs for a specified job schedule with pagination support.
Returns execution history including timestamps, status, and error information.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job to which schedule belongs

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: ID of the schedule whose run logs to retrieve

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description:
Number of job run logs to retrieve. 
If not set and only offset is specified, defaults to 10.
If neither is set, maximum 200 logs are returned.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of job run logs to skip (default 0, zero-based pagination)

get.description: Retrieves the details for a specified job run log

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job to which schedule belongs

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: ID of the schedule to which run belongs

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: ID of the run whose logs are to be retrieved

put.description:
Used by the application to inform the Job Scheduler about the status of an asynchronous, 
long-running job. An application must call this API after completion of the asynchronous 
execution of the job.

**Note:** Job Scheduler passes jobId, scheduleId, and runId as part of the request headers 
while invoking the job action.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: ID of the job to which schedule belongs

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: ID of the schedule to which run belongs

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: ID of the run whose logs are to be updated

success.description: Indicates the status of the job run

message.description: Information that accompanies the job run status
message.example: Long running operation to process employee salaries succeeded

OAuth2ClientCredentials.description:
OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow for service-to-service authentication.
Used by applications and services for automated API access.

scopes.job-scheduler.write: Create and update jobs and schedules

JWTAuth.description:
OAuth 2.0 JWT token authentication (recommended for standard plan).
Obtain token from XSUAA service using client credentials or certificate-based authentication.
Alternative to OAuth2 flows for direct token usage.

BearerAuth.description:
Bearer token authentication for Cloud Foundry environments.
Used internally by CF runtime.

BadRequest.description: Bad request due to invalid data provided

value.message: Error while creating job: 'calmConfig' object cannot be empty
value.detailedError: Error while creating job: 'calmConfig' object cannot be empty

example.detailedError: You are not authorized to perform this operation

TooManyRequests.description: Too many requests - client rate limit exceeded

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds after which the request should be retried

throttling.description: Number of milliseconds by which the response was delayed

ServiceUnavailable.description: Service unavailable - absolute rate limit exceeded

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds after which the request should be retried

throttling.description: Number of milliseconds by which the response was delayed

name.description:
Name of the job. Name must not contain special characters or only numbers.
Must be unique for the technical user.

description.description: Provides more details about the job
description.example: cron job that validates sales order requests

action.description:
The fully qualified URL endpoint to be called when the job runs.
Must use HTTPS for production scenarios to encrypt data during communication.

httpMethod.description: The HTTP method to be used to call the job action endpoint URL

startTime.description:
Start time for the job. The scheduler respects the start time of both the schedule and the job.
Can be ISO 8601 string, custom format object, or null.

endTime.description:
End time for the job. The scheduler respects the end time of both the schedule and the job.
Can be ISO 8601 string, custom format object, or null.

schedules.description: Contains one or more job schedules to be created

description.description: Sets the description of the job

active.description: Determines if a job must be activated or deactivated

httpMethod.description: HTTP method of the job action endpoint URL

data.description:
Data value passed to the job action endpoint when invoked by Job Scheduler.
For PUT/POST methods, sent in request body. For GET/DELETE methods, sent as query strings.

description.description: Provides more details about the schedule

active.description: Activation status of the job schedule

startTime.description: Start time for the job schedule

endTime.description: End time for the job schedule

cron.description:
Crontab pattern for triggering the schedule.
Format: second minute hour day month weekday year
Example: "* * * * * */10 0" (every 10 seconds)

time.description:
For one-time schedules, denotes the task execution time.
Can use human-readable text like "3.30pm", "tomorrow at 2am", "now".

repeatInterval.description:
Used to run schedules repeatedly at some interval.
Human-readable format like "2 hours", "30 minutes", "1 day".

repeatAt.description:
For recurring schedules, denotes the exact time when the job schedule must run.
Human-readable format complying with human-readable date rules.

data.description: Data value passed to the job action endpoint

description.description: Provides more details about the schedule

active.description: Activation status of the job schedule

startTime.description: Start time for the job schedule

endTime.description: End time for the job schedule

cron.description: Crontab pattern (cannot change from/to other scheduling modes)

description.example: cron job that validates sales order requests

data.description: JSON string of data passed to the endpoint

logs.description: Run logs for the schedule (included when displayLogs=true)

httpStatus.description: HTTP status code returned by the endpoint

executionTimestamp.description: When the scheduler actually invoked the endpoint

scheduleTimestamp.description: When the schedule was picked up for execution

completionTimestamp.description: When the scheduler received response from the endpoint

total.description: Total number of run logs available

date.description: Date string in the specified format

format.description: Format pattern for the date string

ANSConfig.description: Configuration for SAP Alert Notification Service integration

CalmConfig.description: Configuration for SAP Cloud ALM integration

enabled.description: Enable monitoring jobs in the SAP Cloud ALM dashboard

tags.name: Run Logs
tags.description: Job execution history and logging operations
